def: adv. With pleasure, gladly, cheerfully, joyfully.
s.m. Stitching; back-stitch; sewing with long stitches, basting, tacking; sewing very thick and strong; quilting; — a stitched cloth

adj. & s.f. Remaining, lasting, enduring, permanent, existing, extant; eternal, everlasting; left in arrears, still due; — remaining portion, remainder, residue, remnant; overplus, surplus, excess; net produce; balance, dues, arrears, outstandings
subs: s.m. Remaining period, unexpired term; remainder of (one's) life
s.m. Arrears of revenue, rent or taxes
adj. & s.m. Owing a balance; — one who owes a balance, one who is in arrears, defaulter; — bāqī-dārī, s.f. The owing a balance, the being in arrears
v.n. To remain, be over, be left, be saved; to remain unpaid or unadjusted, be still due; to have a balance
adj. & s.m. Remaining; residuary; — remainder, residue
(-kī), To find the difference (between two numbers), to subtract; to strike, or carry forward a balance; to shew a balance-sheet
ex: I will throw away the rest.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَاقِی (bāqī), from Arabic بَاقٍ (bāqin), from the root ب ق ي (b q y).

def: = bāqilā, q.v. s.m. A bean, Faba sativa (of Jussieu), or Vicia faba (of Linnæus); pea; pot-herb.

ety: rt. بقل, whence also baqqāl, q.v.
def: s.m. A bean, Faba sativa (of Jussieu), or Vicia faba (of Linnæus); pea; pot-herb.
adv. In small quantity, with scarcity, sparsely.

ety: act. part. of بقر, 'to lay open,' &c.
def: adj. Deeply versed in science or knowledge; — s.m. One who is deeply learned.

ety: for A. باکرة, fem. of bākir, act. part. of بکر, 'to go forth early'
def: s.f. Virgin, maid, spinster.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian باکره (bākira), from Arabic بَاكِرَة (bākira).

ety: said to be a rel. n. from bāqir-ḵẖān, prop. name
def: s.f. Crisp bread or cake (like pie-crust), made of butter, milk, and flour.
s.f. Clasp-knife; penknife.
s.m. Remainder, remnant, residue, balance, &c. ( = bāqī, q.v.)
subs: s.m. The remnant left by the sword.

def: s.f. Remainders, balances, arrears
s.m. (in Arabic and Persian 'a greengrocer'; but in India), A grain-merchant, corn-chandler (syn. baniyā).
s.m. Bark, skin, rind; inner rind, bast.
s.f. The wife of a baqqāl.
s.m. Sappan or red wood ( = baqam, q.v.). s.f. Sappan wood, Cœsalpinia sappan.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بکم (bakam).

def: s.f. House, &c. = bakkhar, q.v. s.m. Syrup; — oilseed.
s.m. Syrup; — oilseed.

s.f. House, &c. = bākhar, q.v. s.m. House; collection of houses in one enclosure; a cluster of cattle-sheds; enclosure, area, court-yard; — a bag or wallet (for tools, — as a goldsmith's); — a kind of drug used as a ferment.
s.f. Side of the body under the arm-pit ( = bag-pātī).
adj. & s.m. Talkative, garrulous; — great talker, prater, chatterer ( = bakwāsī, q.v.).
s.m. Speaking foolishly,
s.m. Confusion; mixture; booty, plunder, prey, spoil.
s.m. = bāqlā, q.v.: (cf. S. vr̤igala, A pea).
s.m. Bark, skin, rind; inner rind, bast.
s.m. Leguminous plant, herb, pot-herb; — baqlatuʼl-ḥamqā, s.m. lit. 'Fool's herb'; purslane.
s.m. Tongue of a buckle ( = baksuʼā, q.v.). s.m. Tongue of a buckle (cf. baklas).

s.f. = bākhṛī, bākhlī, q.q.v. (i.e. bākh, q.v.+dim. aff. ṛī), s.f. lit. 'A young cow whose udder is developed'; a cow or buffalo gone about five months with calf; or which has calved about five months ago; (locally) a young cow or buffalo.
s.f. Boiled grain.
v.n. To prate, chatter, jabber, babble; to rave; to indulge in obscene or ribald talk; to rail (at).
s.m. Piece of blanket, or other cloth, &c. used as a covering for the head by ascetics.
s.f. Name of a green caterpillar destructive of rice-crops.
v.t. To lacerate or tear with the nails, to claw, scratch (cf. bukṭā).
s.m. He-goat.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit बर्कर (barkara, “kid, lamb”) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-).

def: s.f. = bākhṛī, q.v. (i.e. bākh, q.v.+dim. aff. ṛī), s.f. lit. 'A young cow whose udder is developed'; a cow or buffalo gone about five months with calf; or which has calved about five months ago; (locally) a young cow or buffalo.
adj. Crooked, curved, bent; entering on an apparently retrograde course (a planet)
subs: v.n. To retrograde, recede, go backward, enter on a retrograde course.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit बर्कर (barkara, “kid, lamb”) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀇𑀓- (-ika-).

def: s.f. She-goat; goat (generally); (met.), a great eater, gormandizer; idle or lazy fellow; simple person, simpleton; humble person.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit बर्कर (barkara, “kid, lamb”) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀇𑀓- (-ika-).

def: s.m. Box
subs: (or bakas-) wāla; — s.m. Boxman, pedlar, hawker, huckster (syn. pherī-wālā, bisātī).
wety: Borrowed from English box.

def: adj. Astringent ( = bakselā).
s.f. Astringency.
adj. Astringent ( = bakṭā, baksā; kaselā).
s.m. Tongue of a buckle (cf. baklas).
adj. & s.m. Speaking, talking; eloquent; talkative, loquacious, garrulous; — speaker (in general), talker; teacher, preacher, reader; talkative person, loquacious or garrulous person ( = bakwāsī).
adj. Astringent ( = bakselā).
s.m. Iron armour, coat of mail, cuirass
subs: adj. & s.m. Clad in armour, armed; — one who is clad in armour; cuirassier.

def: s.m. Armourer; maker of coats of mail.
s.m. The tree Mimusops elengi (syn. maulsarī).
s.m. = baglā, q.v. s.m. Heron; crane, Ardea torra and putea (in Bengal usually called, by Europeans, 'the paddy-bird,' because of its being commonly found in rice fields)
subs: s.m. False devotee (one whose acts or observances are like those of a crane), hypocrite (syn. kapṭī), pretender; cunning, artful fellow (syn. makkār).

def: s.f. = bakwās, q.v. s.f. Talkativeness, garrulity; nonsensical or foolish talk (syn. bak-bak; harza-goʼī); — s.m. Idle talker, &c. = bakwāsī (for other instances of the use of the affix ās, cf. piyās; miṭhās, uṅghās, &c.).
= H بکوادي बकवादी bakwādī [S. vāk+vādī], see s.v. bak.
= H بکواد बकवाद bakwād [S. वाक्+वाद], see s.v. bak.
s.m. (f. -ī), Prater, &c. = bak-wādī and bakwāsī, q.q.v.
v.t. To cause to prate, to make (one) prate, &c.

s.f. Talkativeness, garrulity; nonsensical or foolish talk (syn. bak-bak; harza-goʼī); — s.m. Idle talker, &c. = bakwāsī (for other instances of the use of the affix ās, cf. piyās; miṭhās, uṅghās, &c.).
wety: بَکوا (bakvā, “talkative, chattery, garrulous”) + ـاس (noun suffix).

r: bakwāsan, bakwāsī, bakwās, bakkī, bak-wādī, bakwāhā
def: s.f. = H بکواسي बकवासी bakwāsī, s.m. (see bakwās), Idle or nonsensical talker, chatterer, prater (syn. bakkī, bak-wādī, bakwāhā).

r: bakwāsī, bakwāsan, bakwās, bakkī, bak-wādī, bakwāhā
def: s.m. = H بکواسن बकवासन bakwāsan, s.f. (see bakwās), Idle or nonsensical talker, chatterer, prater (syn. bakkī, bak-wādī, bakwāhā).
s.m. Word, speech, discourse, sentence (see bāk).

ety: S. बाला, rt. बल; cf. next
def: s.f. Female child, girl, &c. (see bālā); — ear or spike of corn
subs: s.m. A person employed to watch ripe crops; the platform on which such a watchman sits; — (also = ) bāl-rakhāʼī, s.f. Price paid for watching of ripe crops.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀸𑀮 (vāla), from Sanskrit वाल (vāla).

def: adj. Young, childish, infantine, juvenile, immature, not full grown; puerile, ignorant, uninstructed, unwise; — s.m. Infant, child, boy, youth, minor; colt; five-year-old elephant; — hair, a hair; tail; crack (in china or glass, &c.); the thread on which sugar is crystallized; — a kind of perfume or fragrant grass, Andropogon schœnanthus (cf. bālā and bālćhar)
subs: s.f. State of a child, youth, infancy, childhood
v.n. To have hair coming on the face; — to have a crack (in, -meṅ), to be cracked (china, &c.)

adv. Hair by hair, every hair, altogether, throughout, entirely; — by a hair, by a hair's breadth, narrowly, barely; — bāl-bāl baćnā, v.n. To have a hair's breadth or narrow escape; barely to succeed or to win
To adorn oneself from head to foot, decorate oneself elaborately
v.n. To bind the hair; to braid the hair
adj. & adv., bāl-bāṅdhā-huʼā, adj. Minute, precise, exact; skilful, dexterous, expert; — to a hair, to a nicety; immediately, instantly; cheerfully
v.n. To shoot at without missing, to hit the mark; — to have true aim; to have great care, not to mistake
s.m. Children; wife and children, family; — bāl-baććoṅ-wālī, s.f. Mother of a family; — the goddess of small-pox (so named because she carries off whom she will)
s.f. Child-like intelligence, sense of a child
adj. & s.m. As much as a hair, the least or slightest; — hair's breadth, least bit
v.n. To let the hair grow
v.n. Letting the hair loose (a woman); to have the hair tossed or dishevelled; to be in a state of perplexity
v.n. To do the hair; to dress the hair; to shave
s.m. 'Instruction for the young'; a very small book (name of various works adapted to the capacity of the young)
s.m. An offering to Kr̤ishṇa presented in the early morning (syn. mohan-bhog)
Not a hair to be disordered; not to receive the slightest injury or harm
(-meṅ), To have a crack (in), be cracked (china, &c.)
s.m. Pimple, boil or sore (caused by plucking out or breaking a hair of the body)
v.n. Falling off of the hair
s.m. Childish exploits or tricks, juvenile sports or frolics; the sports of Kr̤ishṇa in his childhood
v.n. Pulling or picking out hairs
s.f. The hair on the crown of the head
s.m. A disease that causes the hair of horses to fall off ( = bād-ḵẖor)
adj. & s.m. Hairy, shaggy, hirsute; — cracked; a cracked cup or vessel

v.n. lit. 'To give the hair'; to be shaved preparatory to performing the obsequies of the dead (a Hindū rite, especially incumbent on the son); — to throw the hair of the head forward and backward before the taʻzīya of the Moharram festival (a ceremony performed by Mohammadan women)
s.f. Child-widow, a widow who loses her husband when she is very young
v.n. To allow the hair to grow
v.n. To let down the hair and disentangle it with a comb
s.m. 'Section treating of the boy Rāma,' the title of the first book of the Rāmāyaṇa
bāl-kī kammal banānā, lit. 'To make a sheep, or a blanket, of a single hair'; to magnify, exaggerate, make a mountain of a molehill
bāl-kī khāl khaiṅćnā To split hairs
s.m. Children, family ( = bāl-baćće); disciples, followers — bāl-ghāt, s.f. Infanticide ( = bāl-hatyā); — bāl-ghātak, s.m. Murderer of children
s.m. The act of tearing or plucking out the hair
v.n. To pull or pluck out the hair
(-kā), To tear or pull out the hair
s.f. Infanticide; — bāl-hatyārā, s.m. (f. -ī), Murderer of children
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀸𑀮 (vāla), from Sanskrit वाल (vāla).

def: s.m. Wing, pinion: arm
subs: adj. Expanding the wings; — bāl-afshānī or fishānī, s.f. Expansion or shaking of the wings
v.n. lit. 'To be fledged'; to betray (one's) evil disposition, show what one is made of; to begin one's pranks; to turn over a new leaf; to flourish or prosper
adj. Having one's wings clipped; reduced to straits, distressed, wretched.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀸𑀮 (vāla), from Sanskrit वाल (vāla).

def: s.m. State, condition, circumstances (e.g. fārig̠uʼl-bāl); heart, mind; power.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀸𑀮 (vāla), from Sanskrit वाल (vāla).

def: s.m. Power, strength, might, ability, vigour; force, violence, rigour, severity; effort, dint, virtue; support; military force, host, army; — semen virile; name of the elder brother of Krishna ( = bal-rām); — side, direction, way, wise ( = bhal); — difference (of quality, size, &c.); — adv. & postp. By dint (of, -ke), by virtue or power (of); supported (by), resting (on), on, (in comp.) -ward, -long (e.g. eṛī-ke bal ghūmnā, 'to turn round on the heel'; pīṭh-ke bal, 'on the back,' 'backward'; ćhaṛi-ke-bal jhuknā, 'to lean on a stick'; sir-ke bal, māthe-ke bal, 'headlong, head-foremost'; khambh-ke bal khaṛā honā, 'to be supported on a column, to stand on a column'; muṅh-ke bal, 'on the face, forward')
subs: (S. bala+abala), s.m. Strength and weakness, comparative strength and want of strength; relative importance and insignificance
adv. With great effort, or energy, or ardour, ardently, zealously
adj. & s.m. Strong, powerful; — a strong or robust man; name of Krishna's elder brother Balrām
s.m. Taking violent or forcible possession of another's right
adj. & s.m. Brave, heroic, valourous; the brave, &c.
s.m. The male palm, the toddy tree, Borassus flabelliformis
adj. Possessing strength, strong, powerful ( = balwān)
s.m. Air, wind; name of the elder brother of Krishna ( = bal-rām); — a cowherd
s.f. A species of medicinal plant (commonly called trāyamān)
s.m. Name of the elder brother of Krishna
s.m. A person put in charge of property under distraint for arrears of revenue
v.n. To put forth strength, to make an effort
adj. & s.m. Strong, powerful ( = balwān); a strong man
s.f. Science of forces, mechanics (syn. ʻilm-ě-jar-ě-saqīl)
s.m. lit. 'Destroyer of strength'; phlegm; the phlegmatic humour
adj. Destitute of strength, weak, infirm
s.f. The being destitute of strength, weakness, exhaustion, prostration of strength, infirmity (whether from fatigue or age).
wety: From Sanskrit बल (bala), from Proto-Indo-European *bel- (“power, strength”).

def: conj. But, yet, &c. = balki, q.v. conj. Moreover, but, nay, nay but, nay rather, on the contrary.
wety: From Sanskrit बल (bala), from Proto-Indo-European *bel- (“power, strength”).

def: s.f. Female child, girl, young woman (not more than sixteen years of age); woman; — small cardamoms; a medicinal and fragrant plant, Hibiscus tortuosus (cf. bāl-ćhaṛ).

adj. & s.m. Young, childlike, childish, &c.; child, infant, boy, &c. ( = bāl, s.m.); — a grub that eats young plants of wheat or barley (when about six inches high); — a medicinal and fragrant plant, Hibiscus mutabilis; — a large earring
subs: adj. Childlike, innocent, artless, simple, guileless; — bāle-bhole, s.m. pl. Children, little folk, babes
s.m. The rays of the rising sun
s.f. Youth, prime of life, &c. = bāl-avasthā).

def: s.m. Top, upper part; stature; evasion; delusion; — adj. & adv. High, lofty, elevated, exalted; up, aloft, above, foregoing, before-mentioned; — prep. On, upon, above ( = ūpar)
subs: adv. Apart, away from; secretly, stealthily ( = ūpar-hī ūpar, q.v.)
(-ko), bālā-buttā denā (-ko), To evade, elude by stratagem; to delude, deceive, cheat
s.m. Part of the angarkhā that laps over the thighs, fore-skirt; a kind of angarkhā
s.m. A part of dress; a kind of turban; a coat of a particular kind
s.m. Upper room; upper story; balcony
adj. Higher, superior; having the upper hand; — bālā-dast honā (-par), To prevail (against), be victorious (over), be superior (to); to have power or authority (over)
s.f. Going one's beat or rounds, patrolling; inspection
adj. & s.m. Seated aloft, occupying the chief seat; — occupier of the chief seat, president, chairman; — the chief seat; the upper end (of a table, &c.)
s.m. & adv. The heavens above and the earth beneath; — above and below.

def: s.f. The medicinal plant Sida cordifolia, or S. rhomboides, or S. rhombifolia.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بلا (balā), from Arabic بَلَاء (balāʔ).

def: s.f. Trial, affliction, misfortune, calamity, evil, ill; a person or thing accounted a trial, affliction, &c.; evil genius, evil spirit, devil, fiend; a wonderful or extraordinary person or thing; an awful or terrible person or thing; an insignificant, or vile, person or thing; excessive, fearful or awful amount or quantity (of)
subs: balā-nosh, s.m. One who eats anything vile or unclean; one who eats offerings and sacrifices; one who eats anything indiscriminately
lit. 'Far be the evil'; a phrase used to avert an evil omen
adj. Stricken with misfortune or evil, overwhelmed with calamity
adj. Trying, troublesome; fearful, awful, terrible; an excessive or awful amount or quantity of
adj. & s.m. Tried, afflicted; — afflicted person, sufferer
adj. & s.m. Warding off or averting evil; — what averts evil; expiation, sacrifice

balāʼeṅ lenā (-kī), To draw the hands over the head of another (and then crack the fingers over one's own temples) in token of taking all his (or her) misfortunes upon oneself (a practice among women = balaiyāṅ lenā); to devote oneself for another (syn. ṣadqe honā)

s.m. = balā-ćaṭ, q.v. (N.B. The word balā is often used most idiomatically in a manner difficult to be rendered in English; e.g. tumhārī balā-se, sc. kām hai, lit. 'It concerns your evil genius'; it is no concern of yours; what is it to do with you, never mind
'What awful thing are you?' Who cares for you? You are of no significance
'What fearful or trying work'; what a fearful amount of work
āj to balā mirćeṅ ḍālīṅ hain
'They have put in a fearful or tremendous quantity of chillies to-day.')
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بلا (balā), from Arabic بَلَاء (balāʔ).

def: s.m. Bracelet; armlet ( = bal.)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بلا (balā), from Arabic بَلَاء (balāʔ).

def: adv. In or on the lip, at or to the lip.
wety: Borrowed from English bulb.

def: prop. n. The city of Balbeck in Syria, the ancient Heliopolis.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَعْلَبَکّ (ba'labakk), from Arabic بَعْلَبَكّ (baʕlabakk).

def: s.m. See s.v. bal, 'power.' s.f. Female child, girl, &c. (see bālā); — ear or spike of corn
subs: s.m. A person employed to watch ripe crops; the platform on which such a watchman sits; — (also = ) bāl-rakhāʼī, s.f. Price paid for watching of ripe crops.

def: s.m. A kind of jacket (syn. ćapkan).
s.m. City, town; country.
s.m. Bull; bullock, &c. ( = barad, baradh, qq.v.); horned cattle; burden-bearing bullock.
s.m. Tax on laden oxen.
v.t. To bull (a cow).
s.m. The marking-nut, Semecarpus anacardium (syn. bhilāwan).

ety: for A. بلاغة, inf. n. of بلغ 'to be eloquent,' &c.
def: s.f. Eloquence; rhetoric; — maturity.
adj. Simple, foolish, silly, of little sense, without discrimination; weak in intellect; ignorant; unskilful ( = ablah, q.v.).
s.m. A low caste servant, a village guide, messenger, or watchman; a caste of fishermen and basket-makers.
s.m. A low caste of ćamārs or workers in hides and leather; a member of that caste (he is also occasionally employed to measure land).
= ulahnā, q.v. s.m. Twitting, casting in one's teeth; reproach; complaint, accusation ( = ulahnā)
subs: v.t. To twit, taunt, reproach; to accuse, complain against.

def: adv. & s.m. Additional, extra, unusual, extraordinary; extraneous; general; — in addition to, over and above; — anything extra or additional; extra tax; a horse that is led for show; — s.f. That which is on the surface; cream ( = malāʼī)
subs: bālāʼī paidā, bālāʼī yāft, s.f. Emoluments, fees, perquisites, pickings; bribes
s.m. General, or miscellaneous, duties; odd jobs ( = ūpar-kā kām)
s.m. Superficial, or secret, pleasures; outward enjoyment and pleasure of every kind short of carnal intercourse (with a woman).

def: s.f. Benediction, blessing ( = asīr-bād); boon ( = bar).
s.m. Name of a disease; — name of a forest in Faiẓābād.

r: balaiyā, balā, balaiyā lenā, balāʼeṅ lenā
def: (A. بليه) = balā, q.v.
subs: q.v.s.v. balā. s.f. Female child, girl, young woman (not more than sixteen years of age); woman; — small cardamoms; a medicinal and fragrant plant, Hibiscus tortuosus (cf. bāl-ćhaṛ).

def: s.m. Child, boy, &c. = bāl, q.v.
subs: To be neglectful of (one's) children or family; to desert (one's) family.

def: s.f. A crane.
s.f. = bālakpan, q.v. s.m. Childhood, &c. = bāl-pan and bālavasthā, q.q.v. s.m. Infancy, childhood, boyhood, minority = bālak-pan and bāl-avasthā.
adj. Relating to children; infantine; childish.
v.n. To speak indistinctly (through excessive joy, or through intoxication); to mouth (one's) words; to bubble, boil ( = ubalnā); — v.t. To open.
s.m. Childhood, &c. = bāl-pan and bālavasthā, q.q.v. s.m. Infancy, childhood, boyhood, minority = bālak-pan and bāl-avasthā.
s.m. Childhood, &c. = bāl-pan and bālavasthā, q.q.v. s.m. Infancy, childhood, boyhood, minority = bālak-pan and bāl-avasthā.
s.m. Flacourtia cataphracta; Carissa carondas.
s.m. Lover, beloved person, sweetheart; husband; — a kind of cloth
subs: s.m. A superior kind of rice
s.m. A superior kind of white cucumber (in season in the rains; a variety of Cucumis sativus).
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit वल्लभतम (vallabhatama). Cognate with Gujarati વાલમ (vālam).

def: s.m. Lover; husband.
s.m. Volunteer.
s.f. The lute of Rāma.
s.m. A species of citron = bādrang, q.v. s.m. Orange; kind of citron; a species of cucumber
subs: or A. bād-ranj-būya, s.m. Mountain-balm, Arabian balm; citronel.

def: adj. Possessed of power, powerful, strong; leading an army.
adj. Cheaply purchased, bought at a rate far below the real value, dirt-cheap; — s.m. A great bargain.
s.m. = bāl-pan, bālak-pan, and bāl-avasthā, q.q.v. s.m. Infancy, childhood, boyhood, minority = bālak-pan and bāl-avasthā.
s.m. A particular disease, a kind of consumptive expectoration; the phlegmatic humour.
s.m. Balsam, balm of Gilead.
s.m. A kind of bean, Phaseolus mungo.
s.m. Violence, oppression, exaction; detention of a debtor and violence exercised upon him to recover a debt
s.m. = balāt-kār, q.v. s.m. Violence, oppression, exaction; detention of a debtor and violence exercised upon him to recover a debt
adj. & s.m. Doing anything by force or violence; — oppressor, robber, &c.
adj. & s.m. Doing anything by force or violence; — oppressor, robber, &c.
s.f. The plant Tiaridium indicum; a species of sun-flower, Heliotropium indicum.
s.m. A species of tree, Capparis trifoliata.
s.f. Subtraction (in Arithmetic).

subs: s.m. A person employed to watch ripe crops; the platform on which such a watchman sits; — (also = ) bāl-rakhāʼī, s.f. Price paid for watching of ripe crops.

def: v.n. To ferment (as sour milk, curds, &c.); to putrefy ( = bajbajānā); to make a noise, to low or bubble (as a camel, especially when in rut); to be in heat, be inflamed with lust; to be in a violent rage.
intj. See s.v. bal, 'power,' for all compounds. s.f. Female child, girl, &c. (see bālā); — ear or spike of corn
subs: s.m. A person employed to watch ripe crops; the platform on which such a watchman sits; — (also = ) bāl-rakhāʼī, s.f. Price paid for watching of ripe crops.

def: v.t. To allure, tantalize ( = bilmānā).

subs: s.m. A person employed to watch ripe crops; the platform on which such a watchman sits; — (also = ) bāl-rakhāʼī, s.f. Price paid for watching of ripe crops.

def: s.f. A medicinal plant of sweet odour; a perfume, spikenard (syn. sumbul: — cf. bāl & bālā).

ety: for A. بلدة, n. of un. from balad
def: s.m. A town, a city.
s.m. A name of Bala-rāma ( = bal-dev); — bullock-driver, &c. = baldiyā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Belonging to oxen; — one who loads bullocks ( = bail-ladaiyā, bail-lādne-hārā); bullock-driver; drover; cowherd; grain-merchant.

adj. & s.m. Belonging to oxen; — one who loads bullocks ( = bail-ladaiyā, bail-lādne-hārā); bullock-driver; drover; cowherd; grain-merchant.

adv. Yes, true, even so, well, right
wety: From Sanskrit बल (bala), from Proto-Indo-European *bel- (“power, strength”).

def: s.m. A fruit, the belleric myrobalan.
s.m. Whirlwind.
s.m. Ridge-pole (of a thatched house); thatched covering forming a verandah or porch (syn. baranḍā; osārā).
s.f. Ridge-pole ( = baleṅḍā, q.v.). s.m. Whirlwind.

def: s.m.f. Of, or relating to, phlegm; phlegmatic; corpulent, blubbered; lubberly; — a heavy, sluggish person.
s.m. Sobbing; sob (cf. balaknā).
s.m. Groom, syce, &c. = bār-gīr, q.v. s.m. A trooper who is mounted by the state or the chief he serves, a trooper who does not find his own horse; — a groom, syce ( = bālgīr).
s.f. Roll or cylindrical mass; bundle, fagot (syn. āṅṭī; bhār).

s.f. Ear or spike of corn; — leaf of a folding door; leaf of a palm tree with its sheath.
adj. Mighty, powerful, &c. = bal-wān, q.v. adj. Strong, powerful, able-bodied, stout, robust, lusty, athletic; firm, valid.

def: s.f. Wrinkle, &c. = bali, bal, q.v. s.f. Female child, &c. = bāl and bālā, q.q.v. s.f. Female child, girl, &c. (see bālā); — ear or spike of corn

def: s.m. Coil, twist, turn, convolution, crook, curl, wrinkle, skin shrivelled by old age; the fold of skin in stout persons upon the upper part of the belly or between the ensiform cartilage and the navel; crookedness, obliquity; a bracelet ( = S. valaya); — oblation, offering, sacrifice, victim; the offering of a portion of the daily meal of ghī, grain, and rice, &c. to all creatures of every description (i.e. to gods, semi-divine beings, men, animals, and even inanimate objects); — name of a celebrated Daitya (also called Mahā-balī), king of Mahā-balipura, whose pride was mortified by Vishnu in the Bāman or dwarf incarnation
subs: (S. bali+varkara), s.m. lit. 'Goat for sacrifice'; one who dies in battle, or otherwise, without doing anything
bal-bal jānā, v.n. To be sacrificed; to sacrifice oneself (for), devote oneself (for, or to; syn. ṣadqe, or qurbān, or wārī jānā)
adj. Twisted, coiled, bent, curved, having a turn, fold, or bend; (in Bot.) convolute
s.m. Presentation of an offering to a deity, offering a sacrifice, sacrificing a victim; — bali-dān karnā (-ko). To offer a sacrifice, &c.
(-ko), To give a twist (to), to twist, turn, bend, coil; to present as an offering, to sacrifice
v.n. To turn and twist (oneself), to wriggle, to strut, swell, assume an arrogant or insulting air; — v.t. To sacrifice, &c. ( = bal denā)
v.n. To be twisted, turned, wound, bent, &c.
v.n. To wriggle, &c. = bal karnā, q.v.
v.n. To have folds or twists taken out or removed, to become straight or smooth; to have the pride or conceit taken out (of one), to be humbled
bal-hār, s.m. = bali-hārī, s.f. Offering, &c. = bali, q.v. (syn. qurbān: ṣadqe)
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit विडाली (viḍālī).

def: s.m. (in Myth.) A monkey-chief (son of Indra, and brother of the monkey-king Sugrīya: he was slain by Rāmćandra)
adj. & s.m. Stupid; simpleton, blockhead, dunce.

ety: of bālīdan; rt. Zend, vared; S. vardh
def: v.n. To vegetate; to be grown up ( = bālig̠ honā).
s.f. Vegetation; growth, increase, development inaturity, adolescence.
s.m. See s.v. bali, bal. s.f. Female child, &c. = bāl and bālā, q.q.v. s.f. Female child, girl, &c. (see bālā); — ear or spike of corn
subs: s.m. A person employed to watch ripe crops; the platform on which such a watchman sits; — (also = ) bāl-rakhāʼī, s.f. Price paid for watching of ripe crops.

ety: act. part. of بلغ 'to attain to'
def: adj. & s.m. Attaining, or having attained, to puberty, virility, ripeness or maturity (a boy); of mature age; — a youth who has attained to puberty, &c.; an adult
subs: v.n. To attain to puberty or maturity, to be grown up.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَالِغ (bāliġ), from Arabic بَالِغ (bāliḡ).

def: adj. Eloquent; mature; copious.
(for A. بالغة), fem. of bālig̠, q.v.

= H بل‍ﮩ‍ار बलिहार balihār, See s.v. bali, bal.
adv. In respect (of), in consideration (of), with regard (to).

def: s.f. Pillow, cushion.

s.f. A powerful female; — the plant Sida cordifolia.
adj. Young, childish, puerile; ignorant, foolish, careless.

s.m. Indistinct utterance; affected or distorted language.

ety: Pehl. bakhr; Zend bākhdhi; S. bāhlīka

adj. Of or belonging to Balkh, from Balkh: made in Balkh.
conj. Moreover, but, nay, nay but, nay rather, on the contrary.
ex: ہَمیں کَام نَہِیں، بَلْکِہ مَزَہ کَرْنَا ہَے۔
hamẽ kām nahī̃, balki maza karnā hai.
We want to have fun rather than do work.

adv. According to the requirements of, by the exaction of; in consequence of, conformable to; through.
adv. In the lapse (of); ago.

s.f. Quality, property, temper, disposition, nature, character, conduct, manners, habit, custom (cf. ān-bān, s.v. ān); — s.m. The marriage rite which prescribes a certain number of baths (generally from three to eleven) for the bride and bridegroom
subs: v.n. To take the baths prescribed by the marriage rite (mentioned above)
v.n. To fall into, or to acquire or form, a habit, &c.; to be trained; to be accustomed (to)
v.n. To lay the foundation of character, &c., cultivate a habit, &c.; to educate, train; to habituate, accustom, inure; to naturalize

def: s.m. Reed-shaft, arrow, dart; rocket (for battle), sky-rocket; — the bloody track of a wounded animal; — name of an Asur (son of Rājā Bali, an enemy of Vishṇu)
s.m. The high tidal wave in Indian rivers commonly called 'the Bore.'

ety: S. वान and वाण, rt. वे
def: s.m. Rope made of twisted grass (ḍāb), or of mūṅj (used for the bottom of beds as well as for other purposes).
A suffix signifying 'keeper,' 'man,' &c. (e.g. dar-bān, 'door-keeper'; gāṛī-bān, 'cart-man'; bāg̠-bān, 'garden-er.' The suffix is also written wān).

s.m. The ben-tree, Hyperanthera moringa (syn. sahajnā); — the tree Melia sempervirens (syn. bakāʼin); — the tree which yields Benzoin or Benjamin, Styrax benzoin; — a species of willow, Salix Ӕgyptia (Pers. syn. bed-mushk).

s.f. 1˚ = bāṅh; 2˚ adj. = bām, q.q.v. s.f. The arm (from the elbow to the shoulder); sleeve; support, help; supporter, protector; security, guarantee (as when a person trusts himself in the power of an enemy, a third person, who engages to return him to his home, &c. in security, is his bāṅh); power, strength
subs: lit. 'To have the arm raised'; to be in a position of power, &c.; to be powerful
s.m. Strength of arm; support, ally; a younger brother; — bāṅh-balī, adj. & s.m. Strong in the arm; — one who is strong in the arm
bāṅh gahnā (-kī), lit. 'To hold the arm (of)'; to aid, protect, support
(-kī), To lose one's prop, support, protector, &c.; to be destitute of friends or protectors
(-par), To tuck up the sleeves (for, or against); to get ready (for)
(-ko), To lend an arm (to), to support, assist, aid.

def: s.m. (f. ?), Forest, jungle, wood, grove, copse, brake; cotton-field; cotton crop
subs: (S. vana+vāsa), bano-bās, s.m. Living in a wood, residence in a forest; wild or unsettled manner of life; — banishment, exile; — one who lives in a wood, &c. ( = next)
adj. & s.m. Dwelling in a forest or wood; dweller in woods, inhabitant of a forest, forester; hermit, anchorite; — s.f. Life of a hermit
s.m. ban-billī, s.f. (S. vana+viḍāla), A kind of wild cat, Felis caracal
(S. vana+vihāra), s.m. Wandering or sporting in the woods or forests
(S. vana+bhaṇṭākī), s.m. The wild egg-plant, Solanum melongena
adj. & s.m. Retiring into a forest, leading the life of an anchorite; — hermit, anchorite
s.m.f. Forest animal, wild animal, wild beast
ban-turoʼi, s.f. A wild species of the Luffa (or Cucumis) acutangula
s.m. Name of a class of people who formerly acted as wood-rangers in Gorakhpur
(S. vana+yātrā), s.f. Pilgrimage through the eighty-four forests of the Braj country
s.m. A pilgrim who makes the above pilgrimage
v.n. To beat the forests or woods
adj. & s.m. Moving or living in a forest or wood, sylvan, wild, savage; — any creature, or wild animal, that inhabits the jungle; monkey; forester woodman; wild man, savage
s.f. A kind of high jungle grass of which elephants are very fond
s.f. Wood of the cotton tree (used for fuel, and in the sides of grain-carts)
s.f. Fee exacted by the landlord from the cultivator for permission to collect the juice of the forest date-trees
ban-kaṭṭī, s.f. Fee paid for cutting timber in a forest; the right obtained by clearing a jungle and bringing it under cultivation; cleared land
s.m. Dried cow-dung found in forests (and used as fuel)
s.m. Produce of jungle or forest lands (e.g. gum, honey, &c.); revenue from woods and forests
s.m. Grass used in making ropes
s.m. Land on which cotton has grown during the past season
s.m. Name of a forest in India
(S. vana+makshikā), s.m. Gad-fly; the jungle bee, wild-honey bee
ban-mālā, s.f. A garland of wild flowers (usually of the tulsī, the kundā, the mandār, the pārijāt, and the saro-ruhā or lotus), reaching down to the feet; the chaplet worn by Kr̤ishṇa
adj. & s.m. Having or wearing a ban-māl; one who wears a ban-māl; an epithet of Kr̤ishṇa
(S. vana+mānusha), s.m. Wild man of the woods; the ourang-outang; the Lemur tardigradus
v.n. To traverse the forest, &c. = ban-jhāṛnā, q.v.
(S. vana+utsarga), s.m. Name of a ceremony (much resembling a marriage ceremony) performed in honour of a newly-planted orchard, and without which it is not proper to partake of its fruit
s.f. A female residing in a forest or jungle.

def: part. (of bannā) = bankar, q.v. s.f. Quality, property, temper, disposition, nature, character, conduct, manners, habit, custom (cf. ān-bān, s.v. ān); — s.m. The marriage rite which prescribes a certain number of baths (generally from three to eleven) for the bride and bridegroom
subs: v.n. To take the baths prescribed by the marriage rite (mentioned above)
v.n. To fall into, or to acquire or form, a habit, &c.; to be trained; to be accustomed (to)
v.n. To lay the foundation of character, &c., cultivate a habit, &c.; to educate, train; to habituate, accustom, inure; to naturalize

ety: S. वन; or perhaps the rt. of bunnā, q.v.
def: s.f. Price paid for pasturage; remuneration for harvesting (usually one bundle out of a certain fixed number); quantity of grain given for a day's weeding, or for other daily labour (usually two and a half or three seers; — syn. bannī, banī).

s.m. The woof (as opp. to tānā, 'warp'); a silken thread used in weaving and sewing, &c.; a kind of hemispherical bucket made of iron plates closely riveted together, used in raising water for irrigation.
s.m. A kind of rocket; a kind of sword, a sort of paṭṭā with a thick-edged blade and a wooden hilt
subs: v.n. To be armed; to be ready; to be determined

def: s.m. Stake, bet, wager
subs: v.n. To stake, bet, wager (syn. budnā).

def: s.m. Appearance, form, shape, colour; property, habit, peculiarity; profession, caste, sect, class; veil, covering; dress, garment, costume, fashion or style of dress; uniform, livery; mask, disguise
subs: v.n. To change (one's) dress; to disguise oneself (syn. bhes badalnā).

def: v.n. To open, unfold, streten, spread; to cast; — v.n. To open ( = khulnā); to burst open, split ( = phaṭnā).

s.m. = بانا bānā, q.v. s.m. The woof (as opp. to tānā, 'warp'); a silken thread used in weaving and sewing, &c.; a kind of hemispherical bucket made of iron plates closely riveted together, used in raising water for irrigation.
part. (of banānā), Having made, having prepared, &c. (see banānā; syn. banā-kar, banā-ke)
subs: v.t. To prepare, or get ready, and bring; to settle, manage, arrange, accomplish.
ex: انسان پانی کے بنا جی نہیں سکتے
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀺𑀡𑀸 (viṇā), from Sanskrit विना (vínā). Doublet of وِنَا (vinā), a tatsama. Cognate with Punjabi ਬਿਨ (bin) / بِن (bin), Mewari वना (vanā) Kachchi વિણ- (viṇa-), Marathi बिन- (bin-).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Made, formed, done, got ready, prepared, constructed, composed; finished, completed; fixed, settled, established; well arranged, in good order; well-to-do, prosperous, wealthy; healthy, robust, strong, stout, plump; hoaxed, cajoled; alloyed, counterfeit, fictitious, spurious, forged; artificial, not real, sham, mock, false, invented, fabricated; — s.m. Bridegroom ( = bannā); darling; marriage song; — land dressed and ready for the seed
subs: adj. (f. banī-banāʼī), Ready made, finished, complete, perfect, entire; — banā-ṭhanā, adj. (f. banī-ṭhanī), Dressed out, got up, decked out, elaborately adorned ( = ban-ṭhan)
adj. (f. banī-ćunī), Arranged, set in order; decked out, &c. = banā-ṭhanā, q.v.

bane rahnā, v.n. To remain waiting, to wait, attend, remain in attendance, or at one's post; to continue, be maintained, remain unaltered, last long, be permanent, remain for ever; to do well, thrive, prosper, continue in good condition.
ex: انسان پانی کے بنا جی نہیں سکتے
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀺𑀡𑀸 (viṇā), from Sanskrit विना (vínā). Doublet of وِنَا (vinā), a tatsama. Cognate with Punjabi ਬਿਨ (bin) / بِن (bin), Mewari वना (vanā) Kachchi વિણ- (viṇa-), Marathi बिन- (bin-).

def: (A. pl. of P. bandar), s.m. pl. Ports, harbours.
s.m. = bannāphir, q.v. s.m. Name of a tribe of Rājpūts.
s.f. The violet, Viola odorata.
wety: From Classical Persian بنفشه (banafša).

def: s.f. = baniyāʼin, q.v.
s.m. = ban-kanḍā, q.v.s.v. ban.
